cards.back: A mathematical model for computation that consists of a tape divided into cells, each of which can hold a symbol from a finite alphabet
A finite automaton that can only move to one state at a time and is deterministic, meaning it can always determine the next state based on the current state and input symbol
A finite automaton that can move to multiple states based on the input symbol, and the next state is not always determined
A language that can be recognized by a finite automaton, meaning it can be determined if a string belongs to the language or not
A set of production rules that can generate a language, where each production rule specifies a relationship between two strings
